narration: Most Americans get $3,167 back in tax refunds every spring — and 90% of them spend it all within a month. Here's the psychology behind why.
visual: Dark background. Person silhouette springs in. Dollar counter animates up to $3,167 in amber. Red progress bar fills 90% labeled '90% GONE IN 30 DAYS'. 'WHERE DID IT GO?' fades in at end.

narration: Here's the thing — your refund isn't a gift from the IRS. It's your own cash they held all year, and paid you exactly zero interest on.
visual: Light background. IRS government building SVG. Twelve calendar month boxes sweep amber left-to-right. 'ZERO INTEREST EARNED' red stamp appears rotated at bottom.

narration: Your brain uses something called mental accounting. Paycheck money feels protected — but windfall money? That's labeled 'safe to spend.'
visual: Dark background. Purple brain SVG springs in. Two buckets spring out: green 'PAYCHECK — PROTECTED' with lock on left, red 'WINDFALL — SPEND IT' with burst icon on right.

narration: Studies show people save five times more of that money when it comes monthly in their paycheck versus as a lump refund. Same dollars — totally different outcome.
visual: Light background. Two animated stacked bar charts: left 'LUMP REFUND' mostly red (90% spent, 10% saved), right 'MONTHLY' equal halves (50% saved). '5x MORE SAVINGS' amber badge springs in.

narration: The fix is simple — adjust your W-4 at work. Stop lending the IRS your money. Get your $264 back in every paycheck instead of waiting a year.
visual: Dark background. W-4 form document with animated pencil sweeping across. Before/after: '$3,167 ONCE A YEAR' fades, '$264 EVERY MONTH' in green springs in.

narration: Or if you still get a refund, transfer it to savings the same day it hits — before your free money brain kicks in. Follow for more.
visual: Amber background. Phone with bank transfer UI on left, arrow pointing to piggy bank labeled 'SAVINGS' on right. 'SAME DAY RULE' header. Dark 'FOLLOW FOR MORE' CTA banner at bottom.
